metadata.note: DA, TA, and AA layer outputs were not provided in this synthesis run. Application-layer service nodes are inferred (MEDIUM) from BA capability map evidence. Technology nodes are inferred (MEDIUM/ASSUMED) from explicit stack references in BA documents. All data entity nodes are HIGH — directly evidenced in 05_data_model.md. BA docs were truncated at 3000 chars each; full BA has 39 capabilities and 37 rules — only leading portion synthesized here (see ASMP-004).

capabilities.name: Basket Item Addition
capabilities.description: Add a product to a shopper's basket at current catalogue price, defaulting to quantity 1. Increment quantity if product already present. Create basket if none exists.

capabilities.name: Basket Deletion
capabilities.description: Permanently delete an entire basket by its identifier. Invoked after successful order placement.

capabilities.name: Anonymous-to-User Basket Transfer
capabilities.description: At login, move all items from anonymous (cookie-tracked) basket into authenticated account basket; delete anonymous basket.

capabilities.name: Basket Item Quantity Update
capabilities.description: Update quantities of multiple basket items in one operation. Items set to zero are automatically removed.

capabilities.name: Basket Item Count Query
capabilities.description: Return total item count across all basket items for a shopper, calculated in the database for efficiency.

capabilities.name: Basket View with Product Details
capabilities.description: Retrieve a basket enriched with current product name and image from the catalogue for display.

capabilities.name: Get or Create Basket
capabilities.description: Retrieve an existing basket or create a new empty one if none exists, for both anonymous and authenticated shoppers.

capabilities.name: Order Creation from Basket
capabilities.description: Convert a shopper's basket into a permanent order record, snapshotting product names and images at purchase time, assigning order date and shipping address.

capabilities.name: Order Total Calculation
capabilities.description: Calculate total value of an order by summing unit price x quantity across all line items.

capabilities.name: Order History Retrieval
capabilities.description: Retrieve all orders placed by a specific authenticated shopper, showing order number, date, address, and total.

capabilities.name: Order Detail View
capabilities.description: Retrieve full line-item detail for a specific order, including product name, picture, price, and quantity.

capabilities.name: Paged Catalogue Browse
capabilities.description: Retrieve filtered, paginated catalogue products, optionally filtered by brand and/or type. Note: mandatory 1-second delay in production path (BIZ-RULE-009).

capabilities.name: Single Product Retrieval
capabilities.description: Retrieve a specific product's complete details by its identifier.

capabilities.name: Admin Catalogue Product Creation
capabilities.description: Create a new catalogue product. Restricted to Administrator role only (BIZ-RULE-005).

processes.name: Place an Order at Checkout
processes.trigger: Authenticated shopper submits the checkout form

processes.name: View Order History and Order Detail
processes.trigger: Authenticated shopper navigates to My Orders

actors.name: Guest Shopper
actors.description: Anonymous visitor who can browse catalogue and add items to basket. Cannot checkout without authenticating.

actors.name: Registered Shopper
actors.description: Authenticated account holder who can complete checkout and view order history.

actors.name: System
actors.description: Automated system actions: basket transfer at login, order creation, basket deletion.

actors.name: Administrator
actors.description: User with the Administrator role. Only actor authorized to create, update, or delete catalogue products.

rules.name: Product Snapshot at Purchase
rules.statement: Product name, picture, and catalogue identifier are permanently recorded on the order at moment of purchase. Future catalogue changes do not alter existing orders.

rules.name: Anonymous Basket Transfer at Login
rules.statement: At login, anonymous basket items are merged into the authenticated account basket. Anonymous basket is permanently deleted. If no anonymous basket exists, no action is taken.

rules.name: Order Requires Non-Empty Basket
rules.statement: Placing an order requires a basket with at least one item. Basket is permanently deleted after order is saved.

rules.name: Default Basket Quantity 1
rules.statement: When a product is added to the basket without specifying a quantity, the system defaults to quantity 1.

rules.name: Administrator-Only Catalogue Mutations
rules.statement: Only users with the Administrator role may create, update, or delete catalogue products.

rules.name: Checkout Requires Authentication
rules.statement: Anonymous and authenticated shoppers may add items to basket. Only authenticated shoppers may proceed to checkout.

rules.name: JWT-Based Admin Authentication
rules.statement: Authentication for the administrator API uses JWT tokens carrying the user's name and all assigned roles as claims.

rules.name: Email Notifications Disabled (no-op stub)
rules.statement: Email notification system is not operational. All email send calls return immediately. Account confirmations, password reset emails, and order notifications are silently discarded.

rules.name: Mandatory 1-Second Catalogue API Delay
rules.statement: Every request to the public catalogue browsing API includes a mandatory 1-second delay. Known performance defect in production code path.

rules.name: Admin Panel Product List Cache (1 Minute)
rules.statement: Admin panel caches the product list in browser local storage for up to 1 minute. Create, edit, or delete actions immediately clear and reload the cache.

rules.name: Order BuyerId Equals Buyer IdentityGuid
rules.statement: An order records the shopper's identity using the same identifier as the Buyer account entity (IdentityGuid).

entities.name: Address
entities.hardcoded_demo_default: 123 Main St., Kent, OH, United States, 44240

entities.name: CatalogItemOrdered
relationships: OrderItem (embedded value object; snapshot of CatalogItem at purchase time)
entities.note: Immutable snapshot. Implements BIZ-RULE-001 (product snapshot at purchase).

entities.name: ApplicationUser
entities.evidence: 05_data_model.md (partial — source file reference truncated in input)
entities.note: Extends ASP.NET Core Identity IdentityUser. Full field list not visible due to input truncation (see OQ-005). Confirmed fields: UserName (string), Email (string).

aggregates.name: Basket Aggregate
aggregates.invariant: BuyerId identifies the owner; Items is the aggregate collection; Quantity >= 0 enforced.

aggregates.name: Order Aggregate
aggregates.invariant: OrderDate auto-set at creation; ShipToAddress embedded; each OrderItem embeds CatalogItemOrdered snapshot.

aggregates.name: Buyer Aggregate
aggregates.invariant: IdentityGuid is non-nullable and ties the Buyer to the Identity system.

aggregates.name: Catalog Pseudo-Aggregate
aggregates.evidence: 05_data_model.md (inferred — Clean Architecture pattern; no explicit aggregate root file cited for Catalog domain)
aggregates.invariant: CatalogItem references CatalogType and CatalogBrand by FK. HiLo sequence on CatalogBrand.Id.

repositories.name: IBasketRepository
repositories.evidence: Inferred from Clean Architecture pattern; BasketService requires persistence

repositories.name: IOrderRepository
repositories.evidence: Inferred from Clean Architecture pattern; OrderService.CreateOrderAsync requires persistence

repositories.name: ICatalogItemRepository
repositories.evidence: Inferred from Clean Architecture pattern; CatalogItemListPagedEndpoint requires persistence

repositories.name: IBuyerRepository
repositories.evidence: Inferred from Clean Architecture pattern; Buyer aggregate requires persistence

services.name: IdentityTokenClaimService
operations: GetClaimsForUser (build JWT with username and role claims)

services.name: EmailSender
operations: SendEmailAsync (no-op stub — silently discards all calls)

services.name: LoginPageModel
operations: OnPost (authenticate user, trigger anonymous basket transfer)

services.name: Order Domain Total Calculation
operations: Total() — sum UnitPrice x Units across OrderItems

apis.name: CatalogItemListPagedEndpoint
apis.description: Paged, filtered catalogue listing. Mandatory 1-second delay at line 42 (BIZ-RULE-009).

apis.name: CatalogItemGetByIdEndpoint
apis.description: Retrieve single catalogue product by ID.

apis.name: CreateCatalogItemEndpoint
apis.description: Create a new catalogue product. Administrator only.

apis.name: UpdateCatalogItemEndpoint
apis.description: Update an existing catalogue product. Administrator only.

apis.name: DeleteCatalogItemEndpoint
apis.description: Delete a catalogue product. Administrator only.

interfaces.name: IBasketService
interfaces.evidence: Inferred — Clean Architecture requires interface contracts for all domain services

interfaces.name: IOrderService
interfaces.evidence: Inferred — Clean Architecture requires interface contracts for all domain services

interfaces.name: IBasketQueryService
interfaces.evidence: Inferred — Clean Architecture requires interface contracts for all domain services

interfaces.name: ICatalogItemViewModelService
interfaces.evidence: Inferred — CachedCatalogItemServiceDecorator pattern requires a decorated interface

dependencies.name: Entity Framework Core
dependencies.description: ORM for SQL Server persistence across all domain aggregates.

dependencies.name: MediatR
dependencies.evidence: Inferred — GetMyOrdersHandler and GetOrderDetailsHandler naming pattern is canonical MediatR convention in .NET Clean Architecture
dependencies.description: In-process messaging library for CQRS query handlers.

current_stack.name: .NET / ASP.NET Core
current_stack.evidence: .cs and .cshtml.cs file extensions throughout BA docs; Clean Architecture pattern naming conventions
current_stack.description: Primary application framework. Razor Pages for the MVC web surface; Minimal API / endpoint routing for the public catalogue API and admin API.

current_stack.name: Entity Framework Core
current_stack.description: ORM layer over SQL Server for all entity persistence.

current_stack.name: SQL Server
current_stack.description: Primary relational database. HiLo sequences used for CatalogBrand.Id generation.

current_stack.name: JWT Bearer Authentication
current_stack.evidence: 04_business_rules.md (BR-07): 'administrator API uses JWT tokens carrying username and roles'
current_stack.description: JWT Bearer tokens for administrator API authentication. Claims carry username and assigned roles.

infrastructure.name: SQL Server Database Instance
infrastructure.description: Relational database instance hosting all application data via EF Core.

infrastructure.name: Browser Local Storage
infrastructure.evidence: 04_business_rules.md (BR-10): 'caches the product list in browser local storage'
infrastructure.description: Client-side storage used by admin panel for product list cache with 1-minute TTL.

security.name: Cookie-Based Session / Anonymous Basket Tracking
security.description: Cookie tracks anonymous shoppers' basket identifier. Authentication cookie (ASP.NET Core Identity) gates checkout access.

security.name: Role-Based Authorization (RBAC)
security.description: ASP.NET Core [Authorize] attributes enforce role-based access. Administrator role gates catalogue mutation endpoints. Checkout requires any authenticated identity.

assumptions.statement: DA layer outputs (schema, query models, migration scripts) were not provided in this synthesis run. Data entity nodes are fully evidenced from BA 05_data_model.md, but physical schema DDL and EF migration details are unverified.
assumptions.impact: DATA-REPO-001..004 remain MEDIUM confidence. Physical schema constraints (indexes, FK cascades, nullability) may differ from BA-layer field specs.
assumptions.resolution: Run DA agents (da_agent1_runner.py, da_agent2_runner.py) and re-run foundation synthesis.

assumptions.statement: TA layer outputs (technology blueprint, security findings, NFRs, deployment topology) were not provided. Technology nodes are inferred from explicit stack references in BA documents.
assumptions.impact: TECH-CUR-001..004 are HIGH from direct BA evidence. Full deployment architecture, container strategy, and detailed security posture (TECH-INF and TECH-SEC beyond what BA docs confirm) are ASSUMED.
assumptions.resolution: Run TA agents and re-run foundation synthesis.

assumptions.statement: AA layer outputs (application service contracts, DI wiring, inter-service dependency graph) were not provided. APP-SVC and APP-API nodes are inferred from capability map backing-service references.
assumptions.impact: APP-IF-001..004 are MEDIUM confidence. DI container configuration, service lifetimes, and full API request/response contracts are unverified.
assumptions.resolution: Run AA agents and re-run foundation synthesis.

assumptions.statement: BA layer documents were each truncated at 3,000 characters in the synthesis input. The full BA capability map reportedly contains 39 capabilities; only 14 are visible. The business rules catalog has 37 rules; only 11 are visible. Process models have 10 processes; only 3 are visible.
assumptions.impact: Node count is significantly incomplete for BA-layer. Approximately 25 capabilities, 26 rules, and 7 processes are absent from this synthesis.
assumptions.resolution: Increase BA doc truncation limit in foundation_runner.py (default 3000 chars per file) and re-run.

assumptions.statement: Shipping address default '123 Main St., Kent, OH, United States, 44240' is hardcoded in the Order checkout flow (03_process_models.md, step 6). Assumed to be a demo default, not a production constraint.
assumptions.impact: Forward engineering must parameterize the default shipping address. It must not be hardcoded in generated target application.
assumptions.resolution: Confirmed as hardcoded in source (Address entity). Flag as tech debt in docs 15 (FE Specification) and 18 (Deployment Architecture).

assumptions.statement: ApplicationUser (DATA-ENT-012) source file reference was truncated in the input. Assumed to extend ASP.NET Core Identity IdentityUser as is standard for this architecture.
assumptions.impact: Identity domain entity fields beyond UserName and Email are unconfirmed in this synthesis pass.
assumptions.resolution: Read full 05_data_model.md or run DA layer for confirmation.

normalization_log.description: Basket appears as both a domain entity (DATA-ENT-004, aggregate root class) and an aggregate boundary (DATA-AGG-001, grouping Basket + BasketItem).
normalization_log.resolution: No conflict. DATA-ENT-004 is the entity class. DATA-AGG-001 is the DDD aggregate boundary. Both are valid and at different levels of abstraction. Single canonical node for each.

normalization_log.description: CatalogItem (DATA-ENT-001) is referenced across Catalog, Basket, Order, PublicApi, and BlazorAdmin domains, creating potential domain coupling.
normalization_log.resolution: Canonical: CatalogItem is owned by the Catalog domain. Other domains reference it by CatalogItemId only. At checkout, CatalogItem data is snapshotted into CatalogItemOrdered (DATA-ENT-011), severing the live reference. No duplicate nodes needed. Single canonical DATA-ENT-001 with shared_across_domains=true.

normalization_log.description: Order.BuyerId (string, max 256 chars) and Buyer.IdentityGuid (string, non-nullable) represent the same shopper identity link from different sides of the domain model.
normalization_log.resolution: Canonical: Same value — the shopper's identity system GUID. BIZ-RULE-011 confirms this explicitly. Cross-link added: DATA-ENT-008 references_buyer_by_identity_guid DATA-ENT-006.

normalization_log.description: Two distinct authentication mechanisms coexist: Cookie-Based Session (TECH-SEC-001) for the Razor Pages web surface and JWT Bearer (TECH-CUR-004) for the administrator API.
normalization_log.resolution: No conflict. Both mechanisms are intentional and serve different surfaces. Documented as separate canonical nodes. Forward engineering must preserve both.

open_questions.impact: Cannot validate physical schema constraints (indexes, FK cascades, nullability overrides) against BA-layer entity field specs.
open_questions.resolution_path: Run da_agent1_runner.py and da_agent2_runner.py against the source repo.

open_questions.question: TA layer outputs were absent. What are the full technology blueprint details, NFR targets (performance, availability, scalability), and complete security findings?
open_questions.impact: Technology nodes TECH-CUR-001..004 are HIGH based on direct BA evidence. Deployment topology, container strategy, and detailed security posture are ASSUMED.
open_questions.resolution_path: Run TA agents and re-run foundation synthesis.

open_questions.question: AA layer outputs were absent. What are the full application service interface contracts, DI container configuration, and inter-service dependency graph?
open_questions.impact: APP-IF-001..004 are MEDIUM confidence. Full API request/response schemas and service lifetimes are unverified.
open_questions.resolution_path: Run AA agents and re-run foundation synthesis.

open_questions.question: GR-08 (blocking gate): What is the target technology stack for forward code generation?
open_questions.impact: This is the single gate blocking all code generation. All 20 FE documents are complete and accurate; only the target stack decision is outstanding.
open_questions.resolution_path: User / stakeholder decision required. Examples: .NET 9 + PostgreSQL, Node.js + PostgreSQL, Python FastAPI + PostgreSQL. See project_open_items.md in memory.

open_questions.question: BA layer docs truncated at 3,000 chars. Full BA reportedly has 39 capabilities (14 visible), 37 rules (11 visible), 10 processes (3 visible). What are the missing nodes?
open_questions.impact: Approximately 25 capabilities, 26 rules, and 7 processes are absent from this synthesis. Forward engineering input map may be missing mappings for these.
open_questions.resolution_path: Increase ba_documents truncation limit in foundation_runner.py from 3000 to at least 20000 and re-run.

open_questions.question: EmailSender (APP-SVC-009) is a no-op stub (BIZ-RULE-008). Is there a real email notification requirement for the target application, or should the stub be preserved?
open_questions.impact: If email is required in the target application, forward engineering must provision a real email provider (SES, SendGrid, Resend, etc.) and update docs 12, 13, 15.
